summary: The text discusses methods for extracting letters from words using ciphers, emphasizing that consonants can be repeated or joined to conceal meaning, and mentions geminated letters and positional rules in decoding.

entities.name: geminate letters
entities.quote: preferring geminate letters, which must be placed by indication

entities.name: consonants
entities.description: Can be produced through repetition or conjunction at word ends and middles
entities.quote: they can be produced in two ways: through repetition and through a greater conjunction

entities.name: vowels
entities.description: Must be confirmed by rules before assignment; separated from consonants
entities.quote: do not assign vowels unless all the rules concerning them have been confirmed

entities.name: scripta verbum
entities.description: A method involving scripta (letters) and word meaning to discern knowledge
entities.quote: from that same matter or something else (scripti verbum po[sit] intelligi)

entities.name: war
entities.description: Context in which epistle origin can be inferred for cipher decoding
entities.quote: one can infer from war about which epistle or during the time of war

entities.name: De Occvrr. Lit. Nor.
entities.description: Recipient or reference point in address; possibly a cipher key or title

entities.name: DiGiones
entities.description: Tables referenced for letter presence and arrangement
entities.quote: as in the tables of DiGiones one sees all the letters to be present

entities.name: X
entities.description: Rare letter considered for addition in cipher analysis
entities.quote: either H, or Q, or X, or Z... so that from three places in DiGionibus it becomes

entities.name: potency variation
entities.description: Individual letters vary in power; repetition affects visibility
entities.quote: the powers of individual letters can differ... its repetition will be observed
